title: I love LLMs, I hate hype
titleZh: 我热爱大语言模型，讨厌炒作。
author: therepanic
relevanceReason: 命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: I think from this blog you may misunderestimate how absolutely giddy I am about AI. I did hacking from 2007-2014, after that my whole career has been devoted to AI. I love the progress. I’m so excited for the new LLMs, self driving cars, video generation models, and coding agents. I set up a Linux box with opencode on my local GLM-5.2 last week and wow like just saying install tmux with the geohot configuration works; the Year of the Linux Desktop is finally here!

What I don’t like is two things. One, this constant bullshit about some window closing, or the perpetual underclass, or falling hopelessly behind. This is negative valence hype, not only is it not true, it’s mostly designed to make you feel bad about yourself and move to shitty San Francisco where everything really does suck like how these people claim.

And two, this strawman jump from, oh hey, it’s a fancy autocomplete, smart compiler, better search engine, to it’s gonna like own the whole light cone bro like if you aren’t in SF and at the right parties there’s gonna be like a flash of light in the sky one day and you’re not even gonna know what happened but everything just Changed. I’ll bet you everything I have that this doesn’t happen. The people perpetuating this are terrible people, but the justice is that this is how they feel inside all the time themselves.

Here’s a cool presentation from 2016 about superintelligence. Here’s a movie from 1991 about machines taking over the world. A certain cult likes to claim credit for things that are happening with or without them, and this is my main argument against the valuation of frontier labs. It’s not that AI won’t create that much value, it’s that they won’t capture it.

They try to dress it up with some high minded safety or China bullshit, but the core of the anti open source arguments is a fear of commodification. AI is something that’s happening mostly due to Moore’s law and general progress in computing, not something that they are doing. Of course they have a strong incentive against you finding this out, because then you might not want to give them billions of dollars.
summaryZh: I think from this blog you may misunderestimate how absolutely giddy I am about AI. I did hacking from 2007-2014, after that my whole career has been devoted to AI. I love the progress. I’m so excited for the new LLMs, self driving cars, video generation models, and coding agents. I set up a Linux box with opencode on my local GLM-5.2 last week and wow like just saying install tmux with the geohot configuration works; the Year of the Linux Desktop is finally here!

What I don’t like is two things. One, this constant bullshit about some window closing, or the perpetual underclass, or falling hopelessly behind. This is negative valence hype, not only is it not true, it’s mostly designed to make you feel bad about yourself and move to shitty San Francisco where everything really does suck like how these people claim.

comments.author: SwellJoe
comments.textRawHtml:
This line: "this is my main argument against the valuation of frontier labs. It’s not that AI won’t create that much value, it’s that they won’t capture it."

That is a very astute and concise way to explain everything about how the frontier labs are behaving and how they're trying to push more people to pay token rates for the best models. At the current subscription prices ($100 or $200 a month for a generous, though bounded, amount of tokens), frontier models are a no-brainer, most folks and companies will use them. But, at token rates, 10x or 100x the cost of open models or what I was spending on the frontier models a month ago? That is a harder question to answer "yes" to. I certainly wouldn't spend $1000 a month for the best model, much less $10,000; my employer might pay $1000/month, but definitely not $10,000. The frontier labs need everyone to answer "yes" to spending 100x what they currently spend to justify the valuations, and it's just not going to happen as long as everyone knows how to make these models.

Both OpenAI and Anthropic are trying to figure that out now. Anthropic, in particular, has their finger on the trigger...they want to push people to usage-based billing for Fable. But, OpenAI released 5.6 Sol, competitive with Fable (or close enough), and it's available via subscription (even the $20 subscription!), and there's no moat keeping someone from switching. If Anthropic really does end Fable access on the subscription plans in a few days, I predict a large market move back toward OpenAI.

The market isn't going to bear the cost of making the frontiers investment make sense.
comments.textZhHtml:
这句话：“这是我反对前沿实验室估值的主要论点。并不是说人工智能不会创造那么多价值，而是他们不会捕获它。”

这是一种非常精明和简洁的方式来解释前沿实验室的行为方式以及他们如何试图推动更多的人为最佳模型支付代币费率。按照目前的订阅价格（每月 100 美元或 200 美元即可获得大量但有限的代币），前沿模型是理所当然的，大多数人和公司都会使用它们。但是，以象征性的价格计算，是开放模型成本的 10 倍或 100 倍，还是我一个月前在前沿模型上的花费？这是一个很难回答“是”的问题。我当然不会每月花 1000 美元购买最好的型号，更不用说 10,000 美元了；我的雇主可能每月支付 1000 美元，但绝对不会支付 10,000 美元。前沿实验室需要每个人都回答“是”，花费 100 倍的资金来证明估值的合理性，只要每个人都知道如何制作这些模型，这种情况就不会发生。

OpenAI 和 Anthropic 现在都在努力解决这个问题。尤其是 Anthropic，他们已经按下了扳机……他们希望推动人们对《神鬼寓言》进行基于使用量的计费。但是，OpenAI 发布了 5.6 Sol，与 Fable 竞争（或足够接近），并且可以通过订阅获得（甚至是 20 美元的订阅！），并且没有护城河可以阻止人们切换。如果 Anthropic 真的在几天内终止《神鬼寓言》的订阅计划，我预计市场将回归 OpenAI。

市场不会承担让前沿投资有意义的成本。

children.author: overgard
children.textRawHtml:
Yeah, I've started reaching for local models more. I'll use frontier models at the current cost for tasks that the local ones aren't great at, but when the rug pull inevitably comes, to me they're not worth 1000-2000 a month. And honestly, for my purposes I don't really need models to advance a lot. Like, I tried fable a couple of times and there just wasn't much there to justify its use to me. Opus did the same thing much cheaper.

I think an interesting question is going to be, if models are a commodity, who is going to want to foot the very expensive bill to train them? I'm sure training cost will drop.. eventually, but I doubt it will happen fast enough for any of these companies.
children.textZhHtml:
是的，我开始更多地接触本地模特。我会以当前成本使用前沿模型来完成本地模型不擅长的任务，但当地毯拉力不可避免地出现时，对我来说，它们每月不值 1000-2000 美元。老实说，就我的目的而言，我真的不需要模型来取得很大进展。就像，我尝试过几次寓言，但没有太多东西可以证明它的使用对我来说是合理的。 Opus 做同样的事情要便宜得多。

我认为一个有趣的问题是，如果模型是一种商品，谁会愿意支付非常昂贵的费用来训练它们？我确信培训成本最终会下降，但我怀疑对于这些公司来说这是否会发生得足够快。

children.author: goolz
children.textRawHtml: Yep, at those prices points I am most certainly just going to do whatever is in my power to run local. And to be honest, I think in the near future, we will get to a point where local models will be just good enough that it will not matter.
children.textZhHtml: 是的，在这些价格点上，我肯定会尽我所能在本地运营。说实话，我认为在不久的将来，我们将达到这样一个地步：本地模型足够好，这并不重要。

children.author: lubujackson
children.textRawHtml:
Best comparison is Anthropic/OpenAI are AOL/Prodigy. Massive market capture, no moat. Little by little, the convenience and weight will be scraped off, but they (probably) won't roll over and die for quite a while.

By the same measure, NVDA is Cisco, providing the backbone and capturing a ton of the early benefits, but soon becomes furniture while the excitement moves further up the chain.
children.textZhHtml:
最好的比较是 Anthropic/OpenAI 和 AOL/Prodigy。巨大的市场占领，没有护城河。渐渐地，便利性和重量将被消除，但它们（可能）在相当长的一段时间内不会翻倒并报废。

按照同样的标准，NVDA 就是思科，提供了骨干并获得了大量的早期好处，但很快就变成了家具，而兴奋感则进一步向链条的上游延伸。

children.author: somenameforme
children.textRawHtml:
I think a contemporary comparison is OpenAI's DALL-E. Predating but foreshadowing LLMs they went closed source and tried to monetize it, but within a few years the entire concept just fell apart. Now you can download free open source software, that works better than DALL-E and runs fine on a plain old video card, and for orders of magnitude lower cost.

I think we can start to see the outlines of this happening with LLMs as well. Local models have gone from being proofs of concept to something that is at least remotely comparable to contemporary models, and the gap there is closing faster than SOTA models are pushing it forward. Local models still suffer from high hardware requirements, but so did early image gen models where typical consumer hardware was insufficient for efficiently running them.
children.textZhHtml:
我认为当代的比较是 OpenAI 的 DALL-E。早在法学硕士之前，他们就开始封闭源代码并试图将其货币化，但几年之内，整个概念就分崩离析了。现在您可以下载免费的开源软件，它比 DALL-E 工作得更好，并且在普通的旧显卡上运行良好，并且成本低了几个数量级。

我认为我们也可以开始看到法学硕士发生这种情况的轮廓。本地模型已经从概念验证变成了至少可以与当代模型相媲美的模型，而且差距缩小的速度比 SOTA 模型推动差距的速度更快。本地模型仍然面临很高的硬件要求，但早期的图像生成模型也是如此，典型的消费类硬件不足以有效运行它们。

children.author: nextaccountic
children.textRawHtml:
This will also create a regulatory issue

The US government thinks they can dictate who can access "Mythos-level" (whatever that is) LLMs. But what will happen when this can be run on consumer hardware?

I guess this will be yet another vector too attack open computing and the idea that people can a) own computers, and b) choose what software they run on their own computers
children.textZhHtml:
这也将引发一个监管问题

美国政府认为他们可以决定谁能访问“Mythos级别”（不管那是什么）的大语言模型。但是，当这些模型可以在消费级硬件上运行时，又会发生什么呢？

我猜这将成为攻击开放计算以及人们可以a)拥有电脑，b)选择在自己电脑上运行什么软件的理念的又一个突破口

children.author: darkwater
children.textRawHtml:
> Now you can download free open source software, that works better than DALL-E and runs fine on a plain old video card, and for orders of magnitude lower cost.

Ok, I completely missed that one. Can you point me in the right direction?
children.textZhHtml:
> 现在你可以下载免费的开源软件，它比DALL-E效果更好，在普通的旧显卡上也能流畅运行，而且成本要低几个数量级。

好吧，我完全错过了这个。你能给我指个明路吗？

children.author: somenameforme
children.textRawHtml:
Sure, for a balance between usability and power I'd recommend fooocus. [1] AmuseAI [2] is an alternative that is extremely plug-and-play which can be especially handy with AMD hardware, but is a bit less powerful and also censored by default. ComfyUI [3] is an advanced tool for rich customization and what not. However it's anything but comfy if you're not already quite knowledgeable in this domain - I would not recommend it for a first tool.

[1] - https://github.com/lllyasviel/Fooocus

[2] - https://github.com/saddam213/AmuseAI

[3] - https://github.com/comfy-org/comfyui
children.textZhHtml:
当然，为了在易用性和强大功能之间取得平衡，我推荐fooocus。[1] AmuseAI [2] 是另一个选择，它极其开箱即用，在使用AMD硬件时特别方便，但功能稍弱一些，而且默认带有内容审查。ComfyUI [3] 是一个高级工具，支持丰富的自定义等功能。然而，如果你在这个领域还没有相当丰富的知识，用它可一点都不“comfy”——我不建议新手把它作为第一个工具。

[1] - https://github.com/lllyasviel/Fooocus

[2] - https://github.com/saddam213/AmuseAI

[3] - https://github.com/comfy-org/comfyui

children.author: derwiki
children.textRawHtml: Cisco is also doing quite well with the AI boom
children.textZhHtml: 思科在人工智能热潮中也表现出色

children.author: andy99
children.textRawHtml:
The funny thing about Fable, is we all but know it will be obsolete in a month or two. Between their embargo shenanigans (which IMO they could have avoided just by not pretending it was dangerous) and continuing to give access, whatever marginal advantage it had was essentially wasted.

It would have been an interesting experiment to charge more for it right away and see what the market would bear, rather than tease it for long enough for it to be presumably superseded any time now by whatever is next.
children.textZhHtml:
《神鬼寓言》的有趣之处在于，我们都知道它将在一两个月内过时。在他们的禁运恶作剧（在我看来，他们可以通过不假装危险来避免）和继续提供准入之间，无论它拥有什么边际优势，本质上都被浪费了。

立即收取更多费用并看看市场会承受什么，这将是一个有趣的实验，而不是长时间地戏弄它，以便它可能随时被接下来的东西取代。

children.author: Giefo6ah
children.textRawHtml: They clearly didn't have the compute capacity ready to release Fable at any price that would have seemed "worth it".
children.textZhHtml: 他们显然没有准备好足够的算力，无法以让人觉得“物有所值”的任何价格发布Fable。

children.author: dreamglider
children.textRawHtml: It's not that better than Opus 5.8, IMHO (highly biased since personal) - no more than 10% positive difference for the same prompt, and that 10% is not consistent, sometimes you get obviously better response / nicely done task, sometime you don't.
children.textZhHtml: 在我看来，它并没有比Opus 5.8好多少（纯属个人偏见）——对于相同的提示词，正面差异不超过10%，而且这10%并不稳定，有时你会得到明显更好的回复或任务完成得很漂亮，有时则不然。

children.author: fc417fc802
children.textRawHtml: I don't see how that matters? It's a treadmill. Sure Fable-v1 will be obsolete but the shiny new Fable-v2 won't. Don't view their shenanigans as regarding Fable but rather their current cutting edge model.
children.textZhHtml: 我不明白这有什么关系？这就像在跑步机上跑步。Fable-v1肯定会过时，但闪亮的新版Fable-v2不会。别把他们针对Fable搞的那些花招当成重点，而应该关注他们当前最前沿的模型。

children.author: SV_BubbleTime
children.textRawHtml:
>(which IMO they could have avoided just by not pretending it was dangerous)

Best marketing in a long time though. They fucking called it Mythos and built mythical claims about it. I mean… really hats off to the PR team.
children.textZhHtml:
>（在我看来，他们只要不假装这东西很危险就能避免这个问题）

不过这确实是很久以来最棒的营销。他们他妈的把它命名为Mythos，还编造了一堆关于它的神话般的宣传。我是说……公关团队确实值得脱帽致敬。

children.author: ahoka
children.textRawHtml: Maybe they have secretly hired Kojima?
children.textZhHtml: 也许他们偷偷雇用了小岛秀夫？

children.author: fragmede
children.textRawHtml: Mythos was first publicly known via a leak at the end of March, 2026. Given that Fable is the public version of Mythos, six months gets us September for when the next big leap will happen.
children.textZhHtml: Mythos首次被公众知晓是通过2026年3月底的一次泄露。既然Fable是Mythos的公开版本，那么六个月后也就是9月，下一次重大飞跃就会发生。

children.author: SV_BubbleTime
children.textRawHtml: I love that we can rely on the past being prologue!! It’s super helpful for everyone to have become ridiculously wealthy.
children.textZhHtml: 我太喜欢“过去皆为序章”这种说法了！！大家都变得极其富有，这简直太有帮助了。

children.author: zombot
children.textRawHtml: I suspect they were attempting to move it away from the open market into government contracts where they would have been able to charge more than what the open market would bear.
children.textZhHtml: 我怀疑他们试图将其从公开市场转移到政府合同中，这样他们就能收取比公开市场所能承受的价格更高的费用。

children.author: hintymad
children.textRawHtml:
> But, at token rates, 10x or 100x the cost of open models or what I was spending on the frontier models a month ago

And we can't ignore the power of "good enough". GLM5.2 may not be as good as the SOTA models, but it can be good enough for most, of not all, of our needs.
children.textZhHtml:
> 但是，以代币价格计算，开放模型的成本或我一个月前在前沿模型上花费的成本是 10 倍或 100 倍

我们不能忽视“足够好”的力量。 GLM5.2 可能不如 SOTA 模型，但它足以满足我们的大多数（不是全部）需求。
